goal: Build a macOS menu bar app for tracking PR activity over configurable time windows

target_users: A developer who wants quick menu bar visibility into recent pull request volume by repo.
non_goals: Full GitHub API integration until the popover workflow and data model are proven.
Release signing, notarization, or distribution packaging during the scratchwork phase.
quality_bar: Every iteration must leave a runnable macOS app.
Data transformations must be covered by focused tests.
Swift files must remain under the 300-line guardrail.
allowed_scope: SwiftUI menu bar UI, local sample data, app model, tests, CI, and loop documentation.

completed.summary: Imported scratch PR activity data into the app model, added window filtering, repository inclusion toggles, refresh timestamp behavior, and a stacked chart in the menu bar popover.
Persisted the selected time window and included repositories using a UserDefaults-backed settings store.

open_findings.text: The app still uses embedded sample data rather than fetching PR history from GitHub.
The chart has no hover/selection detail for individual repo segment values.
Settings persistence does not yet handle repository IDs disappearing from future fetched data beyond excluding unknown IDs.

candidate_tasks.title: Add chart selection detail for a bucket and repo breakdown
Introduce a GitHub data provider boundary with fixture-backed tests
candidate_tasks.verification: Fixture parser tests plus make ci-local

assumptions.text: Initial state was generated by autopilot-loop CLI. Charter should be refined by the agent before implementation.
Scratch HTML data is weekly; one-day and one-week windows currently show the latest weekly bucket until daily data exists.

last_verification: make ci-local passed for iteration 2 after fixing strict formatting and a manual total expectation.

judge.reason: The next product gap is visible in the current app: chart values are not inspectable by bucket/repo.
judge.next_focus: Add chart selection detail for bucket and repository breakdown.
